evals.prompt: Write a Snowglobe description for my airline customer support agent. The entry point is chatbot_wrapper.py in the python-backend directory.
evals.expected_output: A 2-4 sentence Snowglobe-ready description scoped to the full triage/orchestrator level, covering the breadth of airline support topics (flight status, rebooking, seats, baggage, compensation), describing users as travelers, and avoiding technical details.

evals.prompt: I want to simulate just the refunds and compensation agent in Snowglobe — write me a description for it. The agent is in python-backend/airline/agents.py.
evals.expected_output: A 2-4 sentence description scoped narrowly to the refunds/compensation subagent — covering disrupted travelers seeking reimbursement/hotel/meal support, the agent's tools (issue_compensation, faq_lookup), and its boundaries (hands off to FAQ or Triage for other needs).

evals.prompt: Help me write a Snowglobe chatbot description for this project. The code is in python-backend/.
evals.expected_output: Claude should ask a clarifying question about which agent to simulate before writing a description, since there are multiple agents in the codebase (triage, booking, seats, FAQ, refunds, flight info).
